CIO Capability Taxonomy (Strawman)

Back Office Functions – Risk, Finance, Treasury & Controls

1. Demand, Portfolio & Investment

  • Strategy & regulatory demand shaping
  • Portfolio prioritisation & capacity planning
  • Investment & funding management
  • Governance, transparency & decision enablement

2. Engineering & Change Delivery

  • Requirements & backlog management
  • Architecture & solution design
  • Build, test & quality engineering
  • Release, deployment & change assurance

3. Run, Operate & Assure

  • Production support & service management
  • Resilience, availability & stability
  • Controls execution & evidence
  • Audit issues & remediation

4. Platforms, Data & Enablement

  • Core platforms & applications
  • Data & regulatory reporting foundations
  • Engineering tooling & environments
  • Security, access & identity

5. AI, Automation & Innovation

  • AI tooling & platforms
  • Automation frameworks
  • Model lifecycle & governance
  • Experimentation & scaling

6. People, Sourcing & Ways of Working

  • Workforce strategy & capacity
  • SI, captive, offshore & nearshore delivery
  • Waterfall, agile & hybrid models
  • Learning & change adoption
Capability-based view – independent of org structure, tooling or vendors

How CIOs Will Use This Taxonomy

From capacity pressure to sustainable change

1-Year Horizon

  • Stabilise delivery and run
  • Release tactical capacity
  • Embed AI into existing practices
  • Protect regulatory outcomes

3-Year Horizon

  • Standardise and simplify capabilities
  • Scale AI-assisted engineering & assurance
  • Shift run/change balance
  • Improve predictability of delivery

5-Year Horizon

  • Redesign back-office operating model
  • Prevent work through automation
  • Continuous control & assurance
  • Sustained change capacity

AI & Automation Lens

  • Engineering productivity
  • Control execution & evidence
  • Decision support & insight
  • Operational throughput

Sourcing & Delivery Lens

  • Internal vs SI vs captive mix
  • Onshore / near / offshore balance
  • Role & skill evolution
  • Cost-to-serve optimisation

Peer Comparison Lens

  • Capability maturity patterns
  • Degree of automation
  • Control intensity & risk posture
  • Platform consolidation
A shared taxonomy enables consistent comparison, prioritisation and roadmap definition
